Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Mostly corporate/editorial voice with occasional human flashes (the '78 days' line, the 'theatre' line).

suggestedPatch.target: Quarterly reviews, now recommended by leading research and local clinics, outperform annual health resolutions by keeping self-care pragmatic and grounded in the specifics of your season.
suggestedPatch.before: Quarterly reviews, now recommended by leading research and local clinics, outperform annual health resolutions by keeping self-care pragmatic and grounded in the specifics of your season.
suggestedPatch.after: Quarterly reviews beat annual resolutions — I've watched clients abandon January goals by March every year since I started coaching here.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Mostly corporate/editorial voice with occasional human flashes (the '78 days' line, the 'theatre' line).

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has two genuinely opinionated lines (Ramadan/theatre, mental-health framing), but much of it is hedged safe advice.

suggestedPatch.target: Long-term adherence grows from small, localised course corrections, not perfection.
suggestedPatch.before: Long-term adherence grows from small, localised course corrections, not perfection.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip the vision boards — one honest 15-minute audit in April does more than any January planning session I've seen.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 6/10: Has two genuinely opinionated lines (Ramadan/theatre, mental-health framing), but much of it is hedged safe advice.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — The 78-day/iftar line and '45°C August' are fresh, but most copy is predictable wellness phrasing.

suggestedPatch.target: Responding swiftly after Q1 is more effective than waiting for mid-year catch-up.
suggestedPatch.before: Responding swiftly after Q1 is more effective than waiting for mid-year catch-up.
suggestedPatch.after: Fix it in April or you'll be making excuses in July — mid-year catch-ups rarely happen in this heat.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: The 78-day/iftar line and '45°C August' are fresh, but most copy is predictable wellness phrasing.

evidence: Point of view vs survey 6/10 — Has POV moments but frequently retreats into neutral authority-citing.

suggestedPatch.target: The WHO well-being model and The Lancet’s review both conclude that regular review increases adherence to health routines and helps buffer life’s instability.
suggestedPatch.before: The WHO well-being model and The Lancet’s review both conclude that regular review increases adherence to health routines and helps buffer life’s instability.
suggestedPatch.after: Forget WHO frameworks — the only review that matters is the one you'll actually do in 15 minutes on a Friday morning.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs survey" scored 6/10: Has POV moments but frequently retreats into neutral authority-citing.

evidence: Quotable sentence 6/10 — The '78 days to first iftar' line and 'fewer March collapses, fewer guilty July restarts' are quotable; rest is forgettable.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — Opening is generic; the strong '78 days' hook is buried three paragraphs down.

suggestedPatch.target: Dubai’s Q1 can quickly shift your New Year wellness goals out of sight.
suggestedPatch.before: Dubai’s Q1 can quickly shift your New Year wellness goals out of sight.
suggestedPatch.after: Your January resolution has 78 days in Dubai — roughly from New Year's to the first iftar. Then Ramadan and the heat take it.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 5/10: Opening is generic; the strong '78 days' hook is buried three paragraphs down.
